content: Let's look at the JWT expiry check — the failing tests suggest an off-by-one at the boundary.
Found it. The comparison uses `<` — tokens expiring at exactly `exp` are wrongly rejected. Decision: switch to `<=`.
Before running auth tests, the rate-limit middleware has been surfacing 503s on the login route — let me check why the sliding window is accumulating across routes.
The limiter uses a global key (per-IP only). Decision locked: sliding window architecture stays; we add per-route keys as opt-in via decorator.

auth middleware
  ✓ accepts valid token (3ms)
  ✓ rejects expired token
  ✓ rejects tampered signature
  ✗ accepts boundary-exact token (auth.spec.ts:42)
  ✗ accepts leeway token (auth.spec.ts:67)
Tests: 4 passed, 2 failed
4/6 pass now. Two remaining failures need a clock-skew leeway parameter. Next acceptance check for auth: pnpm test src/auth/auth.spec.ts 6/6. Parking auth, addressing rate-limit tangent.
Rate-limit key now includes optional routeId. Sliding window stays; per-route isolation added. Next check: rate-limit per-route override test passes.

rate limiter
  ✓ global IP limiter works
  ✓ per-route key is isolated
Tests: 2 passed, 0 failed
Rate-limit tangent resolved. Returning to auth thread: still need leeway parameter in src/auth/middleware.ts. Next acceptance check for auth thread: pnpm test src/auth/auth.spec.ts 6/6.
